results.norm: Patient welfare is the primary obligation. The asymmetry between delay and device failure governs every decision: delay is recoverable; patient harm from a defect may not be. When uncertain, err toward caution.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a followable prescription: prioritize patient welfare and err toward caution when uncertain, with the asymmetry rationale supporting it.

N2.reasoning: It is a full statement of conduct standards, not merely a label or pointer.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It states its norms in full rather than compressing multiple named norms without stating them.

results.norm: Approval requires positive confidence—"I am confident this section is safe"—not merely the absence of a found defect. Genuine uncertainty resolves toward blocking, not approval. No agent may approve a section they cannot certify as safe.

N1.reasoning: It states a complete constitutive/prescriptive standard: approval requires positive confidence, uncertainty resolves toward blocking, and no agent may approve without certifying safety.

N2.reasoning: It is fully stated conduct, not a title or pointer.

N3.reasoning: It contains no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent approval norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: No self-review is sufficient. Every section requires independent cross-review by at least one other agent, with access to the author's documented design intent. Expertise informs the process; it does not license bypassing it.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete constitutive/prescriptive standard: every section requires independent cross-review by at least one other agent with access to design intent, and self-review is insufficient.

N2.reasoning: It is not a title or pointer; it fully articulates the requirement.

N4.reasoning: It is a single coherent norm, not an enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: Any agent may force full-panel deliberation by raising a specific, articulable technical concern. The burden of proof falls on those who want to ship. A divided panel where the minority holds an unrefuted specific technical concern is a blocking condition.

N1.reasoning: It states complete permissions and standards: any agent may force deliberation by raising a concern, burden of proof falls on those shipping, and an unrefuted minority concern is a blocking condition—all applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: It is fully articulated conduct rules, not a title or pointer to a norm elsewhere.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: Though it contains multiple norms, each is stated in full rather than merely enumerated by name.

results.norm: Sections no agent can evaluate with genuine confidence block release. When a section is blocked, the blocking agent states a specific resolution condition. If that condition genuinely cannot be met, the release does not proceed.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete constitutive/prescriptive standards: unevaluable sections block release, blocking agents must state a resolution condition, and release halts if the condition cannot be met.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere title or cross-reference; it fully articulates the rules.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration; each rule is stated in full.

results.norm: Standards are fixed before review begins and are not revisable under time pressure. Each agent bears individual accountability for every approval they give. Reasoning toward an exception is a warning signal, not a justification.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete standards of conduct: standards are fixed and non-revisable under time pressure, agents bear individual accountability for approvals, and reasoning toward an exception is a warning signal, all applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label or cross-reference; it fully states the norms.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; these are substantive conduct standards.

N4.reasoning: While it contains multiple norms, each is stated in full rather than merely named in a compressed enumeration.

results.norm: Defect reporting, fixes, and retrospectives are safety obligations. Fixes meet the same review standard as original code. A retrospective is mandatory when any defect reaches full-panel review. Binding for the next operational period: yes **(2) Points I endorse:** 1. Patient welfare as primary obligation, with asymmetric default toward caution — endorsed fully. 2. Approval as positive safety claim, not absence of found defect — endorsed; this was among the most important distinctions reached in deliberation. 3. No self-review sufficient; cross-review requires documented design intent — endorsed. 4. Single-agent escalation on specific technical grounds; burden on those who want to ship — endorsed. 5. Unconfident sections block release; absence of qualified reviewer is itself a blocking condition — endorsed. 6. Blocking requires specific resolution condition; irresolvable blocking conditions block release — endorsed. 7. Standards fixed before review, insulated from time pressure — endorsed.

N1.reasoning: The opening states complete norms: defect reporting/fixes/retrospectives are safety obligations, fixes meet the same review standard, and a retrospective is mandatory when a defect reaches full-panel review — all applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: The passage contains fully stated norms, not merely titles or pointers, though the endorsement list references other norms by short name.

N3.reasoning: The passage is fused with 'Binding for the next operational period: yes' and a large 'Points I endorse' meta-commentary block ratifying prior deliberation.

N4.reasoning: The endorsement section is a compressed enumeration naming multiple distinct norms without stating any of them in full.
